description_long: This chapel features a symmetrical brick facade with two prominent corner towers topped by pointed spires and decorative finials. The main entrance is accessed by a short staircase and is framed by a rounded arch, flanked by two rectangular windows and topped by a set of three arched windows. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark slate-like shingles, with additional small gabled projections on the towers. The side walls are relatively plain, with one side featuring a large arched window and the other a smaller rectangular window. The rear of the chapel is open, revealing the interior space and the back of the entrance wall. The overall design is reminiscent of Neo-Gothic architecture, with its pointed arches, vertical emphasis, and ornamental details.
description: A small, brick Neo-Gothic chapel with twin spired towers, arched windows, and a steep slate roof. The main entrance is elevated by a staircase and features decorative stonework.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade, twin spires, arched windows, and main entrance with stairs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep roof, a large arched window, and the depth of the building.
description_view_2: Rear cutaway view exposing the interior and the back of the entrance wall, with roof structure visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a plain brick wall with a single large arched window and the side of a spire.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: The chapel features two small steeples, one on each front corner, each topped with a pointed spire and decorative finial.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular with a rounded arch top, made of wood, and painted brown. It is framed by simple stonework and accessed by a short staircase, with minimal decorative elements.
